displayName: Change Authored (notarized assertion, the agent's own word)
description: Notarize-only record of a code change at the moment the authoring agent submits it for review. This is the agent's OWN account: the intent, the claimed change class, what it says it tested, and its risk assessment. None of these are verified by AGLedger or by GitHub; they are notarized so the approver's reliance on them is auditable and so a later divergence (agent claimed testsRun=passed, CI concluded failure on the same headSha) is provable from the chain. The SoR-computed truth arrives separately in ci-conclusion-v1, bound to the same headSha. | diffHash is sha256 over the unified diff (git diff base...head) so the reviewed content is pinned. A force-push after review always moves headSha; diffHash additionally tells you whether the reviewed CONTENT changed or only the commit identity (a tree-preserving rebase/amend moves headSha but not diffHash). | changeClass is an agent-attested claim, not a computed fact; a CI-side path check is the cross-check for misclassification (an application change claimed as docs to reach a lighter review lane). Pure notarization: reaches terminal RECORDED in one round-trip. | This record is FIRST-PERSON: provision the coding agent its own AGLedger identity so it notarizes its own account (in a CI-authored setup, a notarize step in the authoring workflow). If an operator must relay a change authored by an identity-less process, say so in intent and set testsRun to not-run rather than repeating the author's unverifiable claim; the record then reads as a relay, which is what it is.

repo.description: GitHub owner/name of the repository the change targets.

prNumber.description: Pull request number in the SoR.

branch.description: Head branch name of the change.

headSha.description: Full 40-char commit SHA of the change head at submission. Every downstream record (CI conclusion, authorization, outcome) binds to this.

baseSha.description: Commit SHA of the base the diff was computed against.

diffHash.description: sha256 over the unified diff (git diff base...head). Pins the reviewed content independently of the SoR.

intent.description: Why this change exists, in the agent's words. What the approver reads first.

changeClass.description: Agent-attested classification of what the change touches. Drives review routing downstream; NOT verified by the engine. A CI path check is the SoR-side cross-check.

testsRun.description: The agent's claim about local/pre-submit testing. Deliberately distinct from the CI conclusion: the claim is notarized here, the SoR's computed result lands in ci-conclusion-v1, and the pair (same headSha) is the divergence check.

riskAssessment.description: The agent's stated blast-radius/rollback assessment for this change.

revertOfRef.description: recordId of the delivery-outcome-v1 this change backs out, when the change is a revert. Types the what-undid-what edge instead of leaving it in prose.
